message:
perf(mvcc): batch commit-index fence, SmallVec active-commits, proactive chain compaction, lock release iterator, arena write-lock consolidation

Six targeted MVCC performance and correctness improvements.

Batch commit-index fence (core_types.rs):

- CommitIndex::batch_update now issues a single upfront Release fence
  followed by per-page Relaxed stores, instead of per-page Release
  stores. On ARM/RISC-V this collapses N DMB/fence instructions into
  one. On x86 (TSO) the fence is free. Readers still use Acquire
  loads via latest(), so happens-before ordering is preserved.

- Added early return for empty page list and extracted raw sequence
  value to avoid repeated NonZeroU64::get() calls in the loop.

- holder() now checks the fast-path flat atomic array for pages
  1..=65536 and applies the has_draining AtomicBool guard, consistent
  with try_acquire and release.

SmallVec for active-commits (invariants.rs):

- Replaced BTreeSet<u64> with SmallVec<[u64; 16]> for the in-flight
  commit sequence tracker. Under typical concurrency (≤16 concurrent
  commits), this avoids all heap allocation and provides better cache
  locality. Sequences are monotonically increasing, so push() maintains
  sorted order. first() still gives O(1) minimum lookup for stable
  commit sequence calculation.

- VersionStore::publish now holds the arena write lock across the CAS
  loop for chain-head installation. This eliminates a second write-lock
  acquisition for setting the prev pointer and is safe because readers
  take read locks (compatible with each other) and the CAS loop
  typically completes in 1 iteration under low contention.

Proactive version chain compaction (lifecycle.rs):

- After publishing write-set pages, commit now attempts GC on pages
  whose version chains exceed PROACTIVE_COMPACT_THRESHOLD (8). Capped
  at 16 pages per commit to bound overhead for bulk writes. This keeps
  average chain lengths short under sustained write workloads without
  waiting for the hard max_chain_length limit.

Lock release via iterator (begin_concurrent.rs):

- New release_tracked_page_locks() helper and held_lock_pages_iter()
  method pass an iterator to release_set() instead of materializing a
  Vec<PageNumber>. All 8 call sites in concurrent_commit and
  prepare_concurrent_commit_with_ssi now use the iterator path,
  eliminating a heap allocation + sort per commit/abort.

Error classification restoration (fsqlite_executor.rs):

- Restored classify_retryable_busy() dispatch in classify_fsqlite_error_as_op
  so BusySnapshot errors preserve their conflicting_pages taxonomy and
  BusyClass classification. The previous simplification lost page-level
  contention diagnostics.

- New test: classify_op_busy_snapshot_preserves_taxonomy_and_pages
  verifying BusySnapshot{pages="7,9"} → BusyClass::BusySnapshot with
  conflicting_pages=[7,9].

Also updates beads issue tracker metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
